title: Run a Stage 1 Science Fair
overview: Bring together everything from Stage 1 Science to run a science fair that teaches visitors the most important ideas from this stage through real investigations.

project.drivingQuestion: How can we use the science from Stage 1 to run an accurate, safe and exciting science fair for our school or community?
project.finalProduct: Create a science-fair plan with a live investigation, labelled displays, a safety checklist, a visitor survey with a data display, and a short explanation of your scientific decisions.

stages.title: 1. Foundations display
stages.prompt: Choose the two most important ideas from Units 1, 2. Build a display that teaches each idea with a model or diagram, an example and a check question.
stages.evidence: Two labelled displays with models, examples and check questions

stages.title: 2. Live investigation
stages.prompt: Pick one investigation from Units 3, 4 and run it live: state your hypothesis, follow the method, record results and explain your conclusion.
stages.evidence: A completed investigation with hypothesis, method, results and conclusion

stages.title: 3. Connections wall
stages.prompt: Create a connections wall that links ideas from Units 5, 6 to everyday life, with at least three labelled connections.
stages.evidence: A connections wall with three labelled links to daily life

stages.title: 4. Present and explain
stages.prompt: Present your science fair. Explain at least three scientific choices, check that your conclusions match your evidence and identify one improvement you would make.
stages.evidence: Spoken, written or recorded scientific explanation

evidenceChecklist: A safety checklist for every activity
A connections wall with three links
A survey with organised data display

rubric.secure: Observations, measurements, models and conclusions are accurate and checked.
Hypotheses, fair testing, recording and conclusions follow the scientific method.
Labels, diagrams, tables or charts make the science visible.
Decisions are explained using appropriate science words and evidence.

questions.question: What does “Living / Alive” mean?
options: Something that is alive. It grows, eats, drinks, and breathes.
Something that is not alive and never was alive.
A living thing that can move about and eats to live.
A living thing that grows in the soil and makes its own food in the sun.
questions.answer: Something that is alive. It grows, eats, drinks, and breathes.
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Living / Alive means Something that is alive. It grows, eats, drinks, and breathes..

questions.question: What does “Non-living” mean?
options: Something that is not alive and never was alive.
A living thing that can move about and eats to live.
A living thing that grows in the soil and makes its own food in the sun.
To get bigger over time.
questions.answer: Something that is not alive and never was alive.
questions.hint: Use the Unit 1 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Non-living means Something that is not alive and never was alive..

questions.question: What does “Roots” mean?
options: Grow down in the soil. They drink water for the plant and hold it steady, like an anchor.
Stands up tall. It holds the plant up and carries water from the roots to the leaves.
The green parts. They catch the sunlight and make food for the plant.
The pretty, colourful part. It helps the plant make new seeds.
questions.answer: Grow down in the soil. They drink water for the plant and hold it steady, like an anchor.
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Roots means Grow down in the soil. They drink water for the plant and hold it steady, like an anchor..

questions.question: What does “Stem” mean?
options: Stands up tall. It holds the plant up and carries water from the roots to the leaves.
The green parts. They catch the sunlight and make food for the plant.
The pretty, colourful part. It helps the plant make new seeds.
Plants drink water through their roots. In the dry season we must water them so they do not wilt.
questions.answer: Stands up tall. It holds the plant up and carries water from the roots to the leaves.
questions.hint: Use the Unit 2 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Stem means Stands up tall. It holds the plant up and carries water from the roots to the leaves..

questions.question: What does “Can-Do Statement” mean?
options: Seen?
all the parts of you together
the five ways we learn about the world
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Science Words reference.

questions.question: What does “Points to and names head, arms, legs, hands, feet” mean?
options: all the parts of you together
the five ways we learn about the world
seeing with our eyes
questions.hint: Use the Unit 3 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Points to and names head, arms, legs, hands, feet means [ ].

questions.question: What does “Hard” mean?
options: Does not press in. It stays firm.
Presses in easily. It feels gentle.
Bumpy and uneven to touch.
Flat and even, with no bumps.
questions.answer: Does not press in. It stays firm.
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Hard means Does not press in. It stays firm..

questions.question: What does “Soft” mean?
options: Presses in easily. It feels gentle.
Bumpy and uneven to touch.
Flat and even, with no bumps.
Can bend without breaking.
questions.answer: Presses in easily. It feels gentle.
questions.hint: Use the Unit 4 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Soft means Presses in easily. It feels gentle..

questions.question: What does “Push” mean?
options: Move a thing away from you.
Bring a thing toward you.
A push or a pull.
Go from one place to another.
questions.answer: Move a thing away from you.
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Push means Move a thing away from you..

questions.question: What does “Pull” mean?
options: Bring a thing toward you.
A push or a pull.
Go from one place to another.
To end moving.
questions.hint: Use the Unit 5 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Pull means Bring a thing toward you..

questions.question: What does “Sound” mean?
options: Something we hear with our ears.
The parts of our body we hear with.
To take in sounds with our ears.
To hear carefully, on purpose.
questions.answer: Something we hear with our ears.
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Sound means Something we hear with our ears..

questions.question: What does “Ears” mean?
options: The parts of our body we hear with.
To take in sounds with our ears.
To hear carefully, on purpose.
A big sound, easy to hear.
questions.answer: The parts of our body we hear with.
questions.hint: Use the Unit 6 Science Words reference.
questions.explanation: Ears means The parts of our body we hear with..
